characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The middle child and only brother of the three siblings. Dave is a huge, muscular barbarian war-prince who is very sensitive to pain and would rather knit and cook than fight evil. Anti-Hero: Dave is aClassical Anti-Hero. He's cowardly and wimpy but'll pull through when needed. Berserk Button: One of the few things that can actually get him angry is taking advantage of his good manners. Beware the Nice Ones: Normally, he's aLovable CowardandGentle GiantNice Guy. The few times he gets genuinely angry, one learns why it's preferable to keep him docile. Big Brother Instinct: Usually, this is inverted, with Fang often being the one protecting (or at least trying to protect) Dave. However, if Dave catches you trying to hurt her,nothingwill save you from his wrath.

characters.name: Dave the Barbarian
characters.description: The middle child and only brother of the three siblings. Dave is a huge, muscular barbarian war-prince who is very sensitive to pain and would rather knit and cook than fight evil. Anti-Hero: Dave is aClassical Anti-Hero. He's cowardly and wimpy but'll pull through when needed. Berserk Button: One of the few things that can actually get him angry is taking advantage of his good manners. Beware the Nice Ones: Normally, he's aLovable CowardandGentle GiantNice Guy. The few times he gets genuinely angry, one learns why it's preferable to keep him docile. Big Brother Instinct: Usually, this is inverted, with Fang often being the one protecting (or at least trying to protect) Dave. However, if Dave catches you trying to hurt her,nothingwill save you from his wrath.

characters.name: Princess Candy
characters.description: The oldest child of the three siblings. Left in charge while the king and queen are away, Candy has a contemporary "Valley Girl" attitude and would rather shop and hang out than rule a kingdom. Action Girl: Candy's shown to be surprisingly skilled in martial arts and is consistently shown to be (arguably) just as strong as her brother, if not stronger. She may be a princess, but as she often states, she's a barbarian princess. Bare Midriffs Are Feminine: She's the most feminine of the three siblings, with aValley Girlpersonality, contrasted by her buffBarbarian Herobrother and her violent,tomboyishsister. Fittingly, she's the only one of the three whose usual outfit shows some skin around her waist. Berserk Button: She tends to be able to fight and even overpower villains just for doing anything that might threaten her shopping habits. Bratty Teenage Daughter: Can be rather bratty and self-centered as well as just being hard to deal with at times, though part of it stems from her responsibility as ruler.

characters.name: Fang
characters.description: The youngest of the three siblings. Fang's a very rambunctious and uncivilized tomboy who loves to smash things. Accidental Public Confession: In "The Maddening Sprite of the Stump", agiant muffinattacking Udrogoth Castle hurls away the tower where her room is. In her panic, she cries, "All my dollies are in there!", something her embarrassed reaction makes clear she didn't want to reveal. Animal Motif: She denies it all she wants, but Fang does act rather monkey-like quite a few times. I Am Not Weasel: She's constantly compared to apes, and can't stand it one bit. Barefoot Loon: Her insistence on going barefoot whenever possible is especially noticeable compared to her siblings, both of whom wear shoes.

characters.name: Uncle Oswidge
characters.description: Dave, Fang, and Candy's uncle through their mom's side of the family. A rather clumsy sorcerer who usually wreaks havoc with his spells rather than being useful. Accidental Public Confession: In "The Maddening Sprite of the Stump", agiant muffinattacking Udrogoth Castle hurls away the tower where his room is. In his panic, he cries, "All my dollies are in there!", something his embarrassed reaction makes clear he didn't want to reveal. Always Someone Better: Because of Malsquando, he always feels like he doesn't measure up as a sorcerer. Big Eater: Like Fang, he likes to down a lot of food before others. Big Good: While Candy is the interim ruler while the parents are away, Oswidge is thede factohighest authority figure of the family due to being their uncle. In some episodes he tries to be the voice of reason to Candy.

characters.name: Lula
characters.description: Dave's shrill, impatient, sarcastic enchanted sword. Always Someone Better: How she feels toward her older sister, Molly, better known asMjolnir.In one episode, Lula, desperate to impress her, asks the barbarians pretend they're better off. It turns out they didn't need to, since Molly mentions that she left Thor because she wasn't appreciated, and is happy to find that Lula is treated well by the barbarians. Furthermore, she confesses that she was always secretly jealous of Lula because of her status as "the smart one". Animate Inanimate Object: She is a sword with a face that is fully capable of speech. Badass Family: Her older sister, Molly, is better known as Mjolnir (yes, that Mjolnir), and one of their cousins is the Trident ofPoseidon. Cheated Angle: Her face is always right-side up no matter which way the rest of her "body" is facing.

characters.name: Faffy
characters.description: The family's pig-shaped pet dragon. Unlike most dragons, he breathes lightning, he has a prehensile tongue, he communicates in random noises as opposed to grumbles and roars, and he doesn't have a tail. Beware the Nice Ones: Normally, he eats flies rather than hurting them. In "Here There Be Dragons", he's so angry at how Dave and Fang are being treated by the "dragynns" of the Cave of Peril, he picks up one by the tail and engages in a bit ofMetronomic Man Mashing. It should be noted that these dragons are significantly bigger than him. Breath Weapon: He can spit lightning and fire out of his mouth. Elective Unintelligible: "Bad Food" reveals that he can talk normally. From his amusement as he shares Dave's cluelessness with his go fish partners, it seems like he enjoys leading people on. Gone Horribly Right: In one episode, he's turned into a powerful guard dragon by Oswidge to protect against some unknown threat. Because Oswidge never specifiedwhatto guard against, Faffy "protects" everything from everything else, with predictable results.

characters.name: Twinkle the Marvel Horse
characters.description: Candy's magical flying steed. He spends most of his time alone in the stables, and the isolation hasn't been good for him. Cool Horse: A brightly-colored talking horse that can fly. Creepy Good: Has some of the most disturbing lines in the entire show and his presence is unquestionably unsettling, but he's nevertheless one of the heroes and only seeks to aid Dave and his family in fighting evil. Dude Looks Like a Lady: He's a pastel blue horse with a bright purple mane and tail and visible eyelashes, and is adorned with ribbons and bows, yet has the unmistakable voice of Jeff Bennett. The Eeyore:Everythinghe says is either a comment on the pointlessness of life or just plain disturbing. ARunning Gagis that the family mentions theyreallyneed to get him out of the stable more.

characters.name: Dark Lord Chuckles the Silly Piggy
characters.description: An evil megalomaniacal overlord who is also a small, cute, sapient pig with a high-collared cape (and equally high voice) bent on ruling Udrogoth and the main antagonist of the show. Chuckles lives in a castle on the outskirts of the kingdom. Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: Chuckles only wears his cape and the Mystical Amulet of Hogswineboar. Affably Evil: He has a few moments where he shows a more genial side, if only for comedic effect, such as inThe Red Sweater of Couragewhere he thanks some aliens for giving him a ride back to Earth and apologizes for throwing up on their equipment. Beware the Silly Ones: Don't let this lil' porkchop's cuteness fool you. Yes, he's extremely bumbling and somewhat incompetent. Nonetheless, there are several instances where he can be a real serious threat. Big Bad: He is the villain Dave and his family most often contend with.

characters.name: Malsquando
characters.description: An evil sorcerer and Oswidge's rival. He has an obsession with taking over the world and making money. Evil Is Hammy: Once his villainy is exposed, Malsquando goes completely over-the-top with boasting and evil laughter. Evil Sorcerer: He's a very good one, but still evil nonetheless. High Collar of Doom: His collar holds his broach which has more evil spells than Chuckles. The Rival: To Oswidge.

characters.name: Quosmir
characters.description: The god of freshly laundered trousers, overused punctuation and possibly other random and ridiculous things. Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: He's very easily distracted, and actual shiny objects are one of his major weaknesses. Baldness Angst: He tries to cover his balding head with a comb-over, which fools no one. He doesn't take kindly to people mocking his hairstyle. Bald of Evil: Offset by hisBeard of Evil, but Quosmir is bald on the top of his head. Beard of Evil: Has a full beard and is aGod of Evil.

characters.name: Princess Irmaplotz and Queen Zonthara
characters.description: The evil princess of Hyrogoth, a powerful sorceress, andDave's Ex-Girlfriend. While proclaiming her evilness, her plots tend more towards making Dave's life miserable for breaking her heart. Her mother Zonthara is the ruler of Hyrogoth, and keeps on trying to teach Irmaplotz to be more evil. Her father is good, making her half-evil. Card-Carrying Villain: According to Dave, Irmaplotz has "Evil Princess" embroidered on all her hankies. Dating Catwoman:Irmaplotz was first introduced as Dave's new girlfriend, and the rest of Dave's family had to convince him of her villainy. They broke it off, though their relationship afterwards seems to be more bitter ex-lovers instead of proud hero and maniacal villain.It seems to run in the family - Irmaplotz's mother, Zonthara, had a child with a good person, hence why Irmaplotz is only half-evil. Irmaplotz was first introduced as Dave's new girlfriend, and the rest of Dave's family had to convince him of her villainy. They broke it off, though their relationship afterwards seems to be more bitter ex-lovers instead of proud hero and maniacal villain. It seems to run in the family - Irmaplotz's mother, Zonthara, had a child with a good person, hence why Irmaplotz is only half-evil.

characters.name: Ned Frischman
characters.description: A nerd who works at a pants factory in the year 1994. He gains a very powerful zipper which he uses to travel back into time to attempt conquering the world by using the advanced technology of the future, such as the Game Guy, and a joke book from the 1940s. 20 Minutes into the Past:Played for Laughs. He comes from the far off and "fantastical" future year of...1994, which is ten yearsbeforethe series started airing. All of the Other Reindeer: At least at his workplace, he's this, being excluded from water cooler conversations, and making a fool of himself when he does intrude. Evil Laugh: He has one he's spent hours practicing, and never thought he'd get the chance to use. Evil Nerd: Gaining the power of time travel allows his villainous nature to run wild.

characters.name: King Throktar and Queen Glimia
characters.description: The proper rulers of Udrogoth, and the parents of Dave, Candy and Fang. They are away fighting evil around the world, though they regularly stay in touch with their children through letters and Oswidge's crystal ball, which seems to operate like a phone. Ambiguously Brown: Queen Glimia. Battle Couple: They travel across the world battling evil together. Daddy Had a Good Reason for Abandoning You: It's often hinted that Fang, at least, is unhappy about their absence. They had good intentions, given their goal to destroy all of the evil in the world. Here We Go Again!: Just as it seemed like their mission to destroy all evil was about to end, the couple regretfully informs their kids thatnewevil has sprung up in the places they had first vanquished it, meaning their world tour had to continue.

characters.name: The Narrator
characters.description: The narrator of the series. He has never been seen in an episode. Interactive Narrator: Highlighted in "A Pig's Story." Lemony Narrator:The narrator is frequently over-the-top and dramatic, even when talking about very mundane or silly things, and sometimes chides the characters directly or opines about the story.He also resents being called out on this, such as when he dramatically told of the current battle lasting years, only for Dave to interrupt and clarify it's only been 10 minutes. The narrator is frequently over-the-top and dramatic, even when talking about very mundane or silly things, and sometimes chides the characters directly or opines about the story. He also resents being called out on this, such as when he dramatically told of the current battle lasting years, only for Dave to interrupt and clarify it's only been 10 minutes.

characters.name: Dinky and Cheezette
characters.description: Candy's best friends. Ambiguously Brown: Dinky has a dark skin tone. The Dog Bites Back: In "The Princess and the Pea-Brains", Candy makes them her co-princesses in order to have fun while they do all of her Princess work. They get payback by throwing Candy and the rest of the royal family in the dungeon. Meaningful Name: Dinky is short. Satellite Character: They're Candy's friends and that's about it.

characters.name: Mrs. Gert Bogmelon
characters.description: A shopkeeper at the Great Indoor Marketplace. Amazing Technicolor Population: She has green skin. Catchphrase: "We got that!" Gonk: She's green with one red eye, has a long nose with a wart on it, and is overall very unpleasant to look at. Greed: She loves money and will do anything to get it, including trying to sell customers a snake instead of whatever they ordered and tricking them into paying more.
